about.note: The metadata provided herein has been generated using the TFL Designer Community version 2.0.2. The representation of metadata fields available in this worksheet adheres to the CDISC Analysis Results Standard example available on the ARS GitHub repository.

methods.name: Count by group for a categorical variable
methods.description: Count across groups for a categorical variable, based on subject occurrence
methods.label: Grouped count for categorical variable

codeTemplate.code:
in_data = df2_analysisidhere |>
    dplyr::select(distinctlisthere) |>
    unique() |>
    dplyr::mutate(dummy = 'dummyvar')

df3_analysisidhere <-
  cards::ard_categorical(
    data = in_data
    byhere
   ,  variables = dummy
  ) |>
dplyr::filter(stat_name == 'n') |>
 dplyr::mutate(operationid = 'opid1here')

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of all Analysis Grouping Variables, as well as the Analysis Variable (no quotes)
parameters.name: distinctlisthere

methods.name: Summary by group of a categorical variable

referencedOperationRelationships.description: The count operation whose result provides the numerator for calculation of the percentage. The referenced analysis should be the analysis that contains this percent operation.

The count operation whose result provides the denominator for calculation of the percentage. The referenced analysis should have the same analysis set and subject grouping(s) as the analysis containing this percent operation.

display.displayTitle: Summary of Observed and Change from Baseline by Scheduled Visits - Chemistry Laboratory Test

subSection.text: Summary of Observed and Change from Baseline by Scheduled Visits - Chemistry Laboratory Test
Safety Population 2

Abbreviations: Max=Maximum; Min=Minimum; N=Number of patients in the Safety Set; n=Number of patients in the specific category; Q1=Quartile 1 (the first quartile); Q3=Quartile 3 (the third quartile); SD=Standard Deviation.

Notes: Percentages were calculated as 100 × (n/N). Baseline is defined as the last assessment that is non-missing prior to first dose of investigational product. Change from baseline is defined as post-baseline value minus baseline value.
Source: adxx; Program name; Program run date/time
Programming Notes: Repeat for all analysis parameters by each analysis visits as per the results terminology provided.

Parameter (Units)
   Visit

outputs.name: Summary of Treatment-Emergent Drug-Related Adverse Events by System Organ Class, Preferred Term, and Severity

display.displayTitle: Summary of Treatment-Emergent Drug-Related Adverse Events by System Organ Class, Preferred Term, and Severity

subSection.text: Summary of Treatment-Emergent Drug-Related Adverse Events by System Organ Class, Preferred Term, and Severity
Safety Population 2

Abbreviations: Grade 1 = Mild; Grade 2 = Moderate; Grade 3 = Severe; Grade 4 = Life-Threatening; Grade 5 = Death; N=Number of subjects in the Safety Population; n=Number of subjects in a specific category; TEAE=Treatment-Emergent Adverse Events.

Notes: Percentages were calculated as 100 × (n/N). Subjects are counted once for the highest severity grade within each system organ class and preferred term. Treatment-emergent events began on or after the study drug start date up to {XX} days {or study drug-specific therapeutic reach}.
Adverse events were coded according to MedDRA Version {XX.X}.
Source: adxx; Program name; Program run date/time
Programming Notes: Present SOCs alphabetically and PTs within SOC present by alphabetical order.

System Organ Class 
   Preferred Term  
      Grade, n(%)

subSection.text: Summary of Concomitant Medications by Anatomic Classification and Preferred Term
Safety Population 2
Abbreviations: N=Number of subjects in the Safety Population; n=Number of subjects in a specific category.

Notes: Percentages were calculated as 100 × (n/N). Subjects are counted once within each ATC class. A medication can be counted in multiple ATC classes. Concomitant medications are defined as any medication that was taken on or after the date of first dose of study drug.
[a] Concomitant Medications are coded using WHODrug dictionary version YYYY-XX.
Source: adxx; Program name; Program run date/time

   Preferred Term [a], n (%)

display.displayTitle: Columbia-Suicide Severity Rating Scale (C-SSRS) Suicidal Ideation Score: Shift from Baseline Category to Maximum Postbaseline Category

subSection.text: Table 14.7.6 [CNS]
Columbia-Suicide Severity Rating Scale (C-SSRS) Suicidal Ideation Score: Shift from Baseline Category to Maximum Postbaseline Category
Efficacy Population
Abbreviations: <<add abbreviations/acronyms (e.g. CFB = change from baseline)>>
Notes: Suicidal Ideation includes any one of the five suicidal ideation events (Categories 1-5).  Suicidal behavior includes any one of the five suicidal behavior events (Categories 6-10).
Each participant is counted in one cell only. Participants with both Suicidal Ideation and Suicidal Behavior are included in the Suicidal Behavior category.
Percentages are based on the number of participants in the <<analysis set>> with data at baseline and at least one postbaseline assessment.
Treatment
Baseline Category

Source: adxx; Program name; Program run date/time
